chapter_title: Arms and ammunition; parts and accessories thereof
inclusions: Heading 93.01 — Military weapons, other than revolvers, pistols and the arms of heading 93.07 (e.g., artillery weapons such as cannons, howitzers, mortars; rocket launchers; flame-throwers; grenade launchers; torpedo tubes and similar projectors)
Heading 93.02 — Revolvers and pistols (other than those of heading 93.03 or 93.04)

Heading 93.03 — Other firearms and similar devices which operate by the firing of an explosive charge (e.g., sporting, hunting or target-shooting shotguns and rifles; muzzle-loading firearms; signal pistols; pistols and revolvers for firing blank cartridges; captive-bolt humane killers; line-throwing guns)
Heading 93.04 — Other arms (e.g., spring, air or gas guns and pistols; truncheons), excluding those of heading 93.07

Heading 93.05 — Parts and accessories of articles of headings 93.01 to 93.04 (e.g., gun stocks, barrels, silencers, magazines, bipods, sights not being optical — optical sights mounted on or presented with the firearm also classified here)

Heading 93.06 — Bombs, grenades, torpedoes, mines, missiles and similar munitions of war and parts thereof; cartridges and other ammunition and projectiles and parts thereof, including shot and cartridge wads — NOTE: 'parts thereof' does NOT include radio or radar apparatus of heading 85.26 (Note 2)
Heading 93.07 — Swords, cutlasses, bayonets, lances and similar arms and parts thereof and scabbards and sheaths therefor

exclusions.condition: Percussion caps, detonators, signalling flares and similar pyrotechnic/explosive goods
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 36 (explosives, pyrotechnics, matches) — Note 1(a). Detonators, blasting caps, signal flares → Ch.36 not Ch.93 even if associated with weapons

exclusions.condition: Parts of general use (as defined in Note 2 to Section XV) of base metal, or similar goods of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV (base metal) or Chapter 39 (plastics) — Note 1(b). General-purpose springs, screws, bolts, nuts used in arms → classified by material, not as arms parts

exclusions.condition: Armoured fighting vehicles (tanks, armoured personnel carriers, etc.)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 87.10 (tanks and other armoured fighting vehicles, motorised) — Note 1(c). Even though armoured vehicles are military weapons platforms, they are classified in Ch.87

exclusions.condition: Telescopic sights or other optical devices suitable for use with arms — UNLESS mounted on a firearm OR presented with the firearm on which they are designed to be mounted
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 90 (optical instruments) — Note 1(d). Key test: (1) mounted on a firearm at presentation → Chapter 93 (93.05). (2) presented together with the specific firearm it is designed for → Chapter 93. (3) presented separately → Chapter 90 (heading 90.13)

exclusions.condition: Bows, arrows, fencing foils or toys
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 95 (toys, games and sports equipment) — Note 1(e). Bows and arrows as sporting/recreational equipment → 95.06. Fencing foils → 95.06. Toy guns, toy weapons → 95.03

exclusions.condition: Collectors' pieces or antiques (arms or ammunition of historical/collectible value)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 97.05 (collectors' pieces) or 97.06 (antiques of more than 100 years old) — Note 1(f)

exclusions.condition: Radio or radar apparatus presented as parts of bombs, grenades, torpedoes, missiles or other munitions of heading 93.06
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.26 (radar, radio navigational aid apparatus) — Note 2. The reference to 'parts thereof' in 93.06 explicitly excludes radio/radar apparatus, which stays in Ch.85 regardless of its intended use in a munition

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 93.02 (revolvers/pistols) vs 93.03 (other firearms) vs 93.04 (other arms)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for handheld weapons: 93.02 = REVOLVERS and PISTOLS (defined by rotating cylinder or semi-automatic pistol mechanism, not blank-firing or sporting signal pistols). 93.03 = other firearms operating by EXPLOSIVE CHARGE (shotguns, rifles, signal pistols, blank pistols, captive-bolt killers, line-throwing guns). 93.04 = arms NOT operating by explosive charge (spring guns, air pistols, gas guns, truncheons, tasers). Classification sequence: explosive? → 93.02 or 93.03. Non-explosive projectile/impact? → 93.04.

classification_rules.rule: Note 1(d) — Optical sights: mounted/presented with firearm vs. separately presented
classification_rules.description: Telescopic sights and optical aiming devices follow a strict presentation rule: (a) MOUNTED on the firearm at importation → 93.05 (classified with the firearm as an accessory). (b) PRESENTED with the specific firearm they are designed for (same consignment, clearly matched) → 93.05. (c) PRESENTED SEPARATELY (sold alone, not with a specific firearm) → Ch.90, heading 90.13. Non-optical iron sights → 93.05 in all cases.

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Radio/radar apparatus in munitions: always 85.26
classification_rules.description: Heading 93.06 covers munitions (bombs, grenades, torpedoes, mines, missiles) and parts thereof — but Note 2 explicitly carves out radio and radar apparatus (proximity fuses, guidance systems, radar seekers) which must be classified in heading 85.26. This applies even if the radio/radar device is designed specifically and solely for use in a munition. The 'parts thereof' scope of 93.06 simply does not extend to radio/radar apparatus.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.93 (functional arms) vs Ch.95 (sporting/toy) vs Ch.97 (antiques/collectors)
classification_rules.description: Three competing chapters based on nature/purpose: Ch.93 = functional arms and ammunition designed for actual use as weapons. Ch.95 = bows/arrows/fencing foils as sports equipment (95.06); toy weapons (95.03). Ch.97 = arms of historical/collectible value (97.05 or 97.06). Test sequence: (1) Is it functional as a weapon? → Ch.93. (2) Is it purely for sport (no lethality intended)? → Ch.95. (3) Is it a collector's piece or antique? → Ch.97. Decorative swords that are functional → Ch.93 (93.07).

classification_rules.rule: Heading 93.07 — edged weapons: swords, bayonets, lances and similar
classification_rules.description: Heading 93.07 covers bladed/pointed weapons: swords, cutlasses, bayonets, lances and 'similar arms'. Includes scabbards and sheaths. The 'similar arms' language is broad enough to include daggers, machetes when designed as weapons, combat knives. Folding knives and utility knives → typically Ch.82 (cutlery). Ceremonial swords (functional) → 93.07. Toy swords → 95.03.

Parts and accessories of headings 93.01–93.04 classified in 93.05 include: gun barrels, stocks, bolts, triggers, magazines, silencers/suppressors, bipods/tripods for firearms, non-optical iron sights, optical sights when mounted on/presented with the firearm (per Note 1(d)), grips, handguards. General-purpose parts of base metal → Section XV per Note 1(b). Optical sights presented separately → Ch.90.
